page.content: The stakeholder analysis reveals the following: for Physicians, the knowledge of stakeholder regarding the initiative is Medium, their interest in the issue (willingness to initiate or lead) is Low, their influence/power is High, level of engagement is Involve, the impact of the issue on the actor is High, and the proposed engagement method is Increase ownership/workshops/meetings. Nurses have a Low knowledge of stakeholder regarding the initiative, a Medium interest in the issue, Medium influence/power, an Involve level of engagement, Medium impact of the issue, and proposed engagement method is Workshops/meetings. For Patient/patient's family, knowledge is Medium, interest is High, influence/power is Low, engagement is Involve, impact is High, with proposed engagement method being One on one meetings. Pharmacy stakeholders have Low knowledge, Low interest, High influence/power, an Involve engagement level, Medium impact, and the engagement method is Electronic system. The Accounting department shows Low knowledge, Low interest, Medium influence/power, a Collaborate engagement level, Medium impact, and proposed engagement method is Electronic system. Medical records have Medium knowledge, Medium interest, Medium influence/power, a Collaborate engagement level, Medium impact, and proposed engagement method is Electronic system. In Table 2, the Age at participation for BRCA1 mutation carriers is 40.2 (8.1), for BRCA2 mutation carriers is 39.5 (13.7), for non-carriers with family history of breast cancer is 46.3 (11.1), and for non-carriers with early-onset breast cancer is 38.8 (13.1). The table also shows that among postmenopausal women, BRCA1 mutation carriers are 40 (23.8), BRCA2 mutation carriers are 13 (24.1), non-carriers with family history are 112 (44.8), and non-carriers with early-onset breast cancer are 19 (17.4). Current drinkers in BRCA1 mutation carriers are 32 (19.1), in BRCA2 mutation carriers are 17 (31.5), in non-carriers with family history are 42 (16.8), and in non-carriers with early-onset breast cancer are 45 (41.3). Current smokers include 7 (4.2) in BRCA1 mutation carriers, 5 (9.3) in BRCA2 mutation carriers, 4 (1.6) in non-carriers with family history, and 2 (1.8) in non-carriers with early-onset breast cancer. For BMI ≥ 25 Kg/m2, BRCA1 mutation carriers have 28 (16.8), BRCA2 mutation carriers have 8 (14.8), non-carriers with family history are 46 (18.4), and non-carriers with early-onset breast cancer are 23 (21.1).
